Literally "once"; used as a double enclitic to soften a verb or imply short duration.
used for emphasis, to state a consequence, or to refer to something previously mentioned.
Book POJ: chō-ji̍t. Chō does not change tone; ji̍t is enclitic. tōa-chō-ji̍t = 3 days ago.
Interior capability of performing specified actions. Negative: bē.
At end of phrase/clause/sentence, tit is enclitic and the preceding word (ēng/sái/iōng) does not change tone. Negative: change ē to bē.

english: passive marker: hō· + Agent + Verb — the subject receives the action performed by the agent

notes: Includes things like rat poison. Measure determined by the form of the medicine.

taiwanese_hanji: 一/二/三/四/五/六/七/八/九/十/零
mandarin: 一/二/三/四/五/六/七/八/九/十/零
english: literary readings of the numbers 1–10 and zero
notes: Used in phone numbers, years, and other identification terms. khòng = alternative for zero. lio̍k (6) and si̍p (10) are tone 8.

english: object marker: kā + Verb marks the verb's direct object
notes: Without change of tone. Substitutes for kā i (the "i" = him/her/it), like an English direct/indirect object.

english: labor / a day's work; a day
notes: Used of manual / blue-collar work, not white-collar; but often means an ordinary "day" = ji̍t.
Measure Word: keng / ê.
Measure Word: hāng.

english: Verb + khì — expresses the loss of something caused by the action of the verb
notes: The tones on the verb and on -khì do not change.

english: khì-hō· + Agent + Verb + khì — the agent goes and does (the verb) to the subject (adversative passive)
notes: The tones on the verb and on the following khì do not change.

english: regard as / consider as / treat as
notes: Usually mistakenly.

english: to see / happen to see / get to see
notes: At end of phrase/clause/sentence, -kìⁿ and -tio̍h are enclitic.

english: to see and understand / read and understand
notes: Opposite: khòaⁿ-bô.
